description_long: This is a vintage rangefinder camera, likely a Soviet-era Zorki-4, featuring a robust metal body with a black textured grip and silver metallic top and bottom plates. The front is dominated by a large, protruding lens with engraved markings, and a distinctive turret-style viewfinder attachment sits atop the camera, equipped with multiple optical elements. The top panel includes several dials and buttons for adjusting settings, as well as a film advance lever and a shutter release button. The back of the camera is relatively plain, with a film door and a small window for viewing the film type or frame count. The sides reveal the cylindrical lens barrel and the mechanical controls, while the overall construction suggests durability and precision typical of mid-20th-century cameras. The camera's design is utilitarian, with visible wear and patina indicating extensive use.
description: A vintage Zorki-4 rangefinder camera with a large lens, turret viewfinder, and a sturdy metal body with black and silver finishes.
description_view_0: Front view showing the prominent lens, turret viewfinder, and engraved brand name on the top plate.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the lens barrel, side grip, and the profile of the turret viewfinder.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain back panel, film door, and the rear of the turret viewfinder.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the dials, buttons, and the cylindrical lens protruding from the body.
